title: Jobs harder to find but AI not to blame
full_text:
Australians will find it harder to get a new job in 2026 but probably not because of artificial intelligence, according to a Deloitte Access Economics report.
Based on a study of 82 roles, dubbed “AI-disrupted jobs” due to not needing human judgment, empathy or interpersonal skills, Deloitte found employment was still rising in these sectors.
It said AI was “currently playing more of an augmentative role in the Australian labour market, with Australians less likely to use AI primarily for automation”.
It said, however, that it would still be harder for Australians to find a new role after three interest rate hikes and the economic impacts of the Middle East war.
Annual employment growth slowed to 0.9 per cent in the year to April 2026, down from a 1.9 per cent average over the previous three years.
Unemployment has risen 0.4 percentage points since December 2025. “With economic uncertainty rising, businesses are expected to behave more cautiously, tempering hiring decisions,” the firm said.
